Francis Godwin, Director, Office of Manufacturing Quality (Office of Compliance, CDER); and Tina Smith, Captain, U.S. Public Health Service, Director, Office of Unapproved Drugs & Labeling Compliance (Office of Compliance, CDER).

15 working days.
Inspection start (September 29, 2025) → Inspection end (October 16, 2025) → Firm's 483 response (November 6, 2025) → Warning-letter date (April 9, 2026).
MARCS-CMS 721916 (page header) and Warning Letter 320-26-61 (body header).
Finished Drug Product Microbiological Testing; Stability Assay; Complaint Investigations.
Plexion Cream and Plexion Lotion.
Indicated for the topical treatment and removal of common warts and plantar warts.
Reply by email to CDER-OC-OMQ-Communications@fda.hhs.gov; ATTN: Andrew Haack; identify the response with FEI 2513595.
Gradual bleaching of hyperpigmented skin conditions such as chloasma, melasma, freckles, senile lentigines, and areas of melanin hyperpigmentation.
Violation 2 — 21 CFR 211.100(a), production and process control.

No. The Responsibilities as a Contractor section states FDA regards contractors as extensions of the manufacturer, and the firm is responsible for the quality of drugs it produces regardless of agreements in place with product owners.

The consultant should (1) evaluate the firm's operations and assist it in meeting CGMP requirements, and (2) perform a comprehensive six-system audit of the entire operation for CGMP compliance and evaluate the completion and efficacy of corrective actions and preventive actions before the firm pursues resolution of its compliance status with FDA.

No — the specific lot identifiers, products, and CFU numbers are redacted as (b)(4). Only one entry partially shows 'TYMC: (b)(4) colony forming units (CFU) per gram.' The numeric values themselves are not in the source.

The risk assessment did not extend to all drug products where the (b)(4) treatment was attempted, did not address product quality and patient safety risks for lots on which (b)(4) treatment was used or product released after failing microbiological results, did not address whether additional bacterial species were present, and did not provide evidence supporting that growth on total yeast and mold plates was solely bacterial.
No. The firm committed to revising the complaint and investigation procedures but did not provide the revised procedures.
No. The letter states no approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act are in effect for these products, and therefore they are unapproved new drugs.
No. FDA notes the firm reduced the labeled temperature limits, but states they were still outside the range supported by the firm's stability data.
